title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a talk about volunteering at a wildlife park.
audio_script: Speaker: Hello, and thank you for coming to this short talk about volunteering at Green Valley Wildlife Park. We need helpers during the school holidays, especially at weekends when many families visit. You do not have to know a lot about animals, but you must enjoy talking to visitors and working outside. The person who will organise the volunteers this summer is Mr [14]Harper[/14]. He has worked here for twelve years and will show you exactly what to do. On your first morning, please arrive at [15]eight fifteen[/15], because the park opens to visitors at nine. We will give you a jacket, but you should wear comfortable [16]boots[/16], as some paths can be muddy after rain. Please also bring your lunch, although drinks are available in the cafe. Most new volunteers begin in the small animal area, where they help feed the [17]rabbits[/17] and answer children's questions. You will not work with dangerous animals, so please do not worry about that. During breaks, volunteers can use the [18]staff room[/18], which has drinks, lockers and a microwave. There will always be an experienced member of staff nearby, and nobody is asked to work alone. We ask everyone to help for at least four days, but many people stay longer because they enjoy it. If you complete the training, you will receive a free family ticket worth [19]thirty[/19] pounds. Please take an application form from the table at the back before you go. If you are accepted, you will come to one short training session the week before the holidays start. At this session, we will talk about safety rules and practise answering the most common visitor questions. Please bring a pen, because there will be a simple form to complete.

questions.explanation: The speaker says Mr Harper will organise the volunteers this summer.
The speaker says volunteers should arrive at eight fifteen on the first morning.
The speaker says volunteers should wear comfortable boots.
The speaker says new volunteers help feed the rabbits.
The speaker says volunteers can use the staff room during breaks.
The speaker says the free family ticket is worth thirty pounds.
